item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should "stay of eexecution" be "stay of termination"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to comment on the similarities1 between this and the toaster, but then i read the Author Post, and then I smiled, and then I upvoted.

Footnotes
1. Or lack there of. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this:

All data on SCP-1040 is to be corrected electronically through the use of project Null Context to ensure that the object's effects do not interfere with normal documentation procedures.

Then, +1
Fuck you I'm no toaster nigga.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea behind Null Context was that it was natural language processing software that could identify and correct aberrant referrants in documentation like this article, developed to prevent more articles like that goddamned toaster. I never explored the idea further but would enjoy seeing it used.

In real life, I came up with it because I knew I didn't want this to be a format screw in addition to all of the other things it already is…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the object and the effect, but personally I upvoted for Project Null Context. I want a tale about those technicians- the ones who work on documentation for SCPs they've never seen, and keep having to explain the point of their work to everyone who has been exposed and is affected, and can't understand why their documentation needs to be changed…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is super cute, and also a great crossover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Leaving this here so I don't keep losing track of it:
https://mobile.twitter.com/hotaruri/status/711049248087744512

I have no idea what that says but the fanart made me tremendousdy happy.

item.title: Re: Damn Daniel
item.content:encoded: I think it's the fact that the SCPs name is called Daniel but still that MEEEMMMMM.

item.title: Re: Damn Daniel
item.content:encoded: What does this have to do with the SCP even

item.title: Damn Daniel
item.content:encoded: DAAAAAAAAAAAAAMN DANIEL BACK AT IT AGAIN WITH THE WHITE VANS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little late to be replying on this, but look closely at other names in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I don't get it. Could you explain that one to me, please?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. But I do kind of wish that the two O5-level approvals were just Site Director ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The baby flashlights are dawwsome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that the detail that the previous owner's name had six letters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was also reminded of me very quickly, but this is very neatly done and also inverts my scare factor quite nicely.

I also upvote because this reminds me nicely of the effects of various hallucinogens, in respect to the thinking-something-is-alive-but-still-kind-of-knowing-it-isn't. Maybe you could add a small note to this effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the fantastically bizarre mental image of a pair of floor lamps lying sideways on a bed, and the train of thought the person placing them that way must have been experiencing. That's the kind of quirky oddness that's quintissential for good non-creepy SPCs in my mind.

Also, noted the similarity to the Toaster immediately (before I read this was intentionally inspired by it), and I felt the article was good enough to lived up to the comparison.

+1

(Also, the knitting the sun a sweater comment made me d'awwwww)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … that's adorable…. xD

item.title: Re: Picture
item.content:encoded: I did spend some time poking around for a picture, and had several possibilities in mind (including that one, incidentally). However, I didn't include one because it would change the flow of the article, albeit not as much as it would have for SCP-426. Thank you though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted at "bassinets."

item.title: Re: Picture
item.content:encoded: If Lumancer, the author, feels adding a picture is a good idea, go ahead. But you shouldn't do anything until you have his express permission.

item.title: Picture
item.content:encoded:
I was just wondering if I could add a picture to this? It doesn't need it, but I tend to like articles more when I have something to go off. Also, I looked over the whole antique stained glass thing and started visualising modern lamps XD

http://ak.buy.com/PI/0/500/226271393.jpg
This is just an example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could get a few more clever cross-testing sugdestions I'd do so happily, but none come to mind =(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's a bit bril.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

A++. I love it.

Pity it's not in the main article (though I see why you wouldn't want it to be, short as it is).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really hoping to answer this by posting a full old-timey experiment log for Daniel, complete with rampant cross-testing. Unfortunately, I haven't been able to come up with nearly enough material I was happy with. So here's your answer, albeit three weeks late:

+ 1040 experiment
- 1040 experiment

Date: ██-██-████
Subject: SCP-890
Intent: Investigation of object's perceptual effect on a subject with abnormal perception of similar objects
Procedure: Subject introduced to the testing without any medical equipment to ensure safety of the object. Plexiglass barrier errected in testing chamber to insure no physical interaction between object and subject.
Results: Subject silently observes SCP-1040 for approximately 5 minutes. Subject then stated to attending researcher: "I'm sorry, but I'm a medical doctor. This man needs a psychiatrist."
Notes: none

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And another upvote from me. This has a lot of charm to it, and it's executed very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you just made me smile in my heartmeats

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, how could you have ever doubted the bees? D=

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My two highest rated things are both things I expected to get downvoted into oblivion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
with a trifecta of:

…the owner's cause of death (heart issues exacerbated by electrocution).

and

…Subject develops increasingly elaborate relationships with anthropomorphized objects, including… romantic interest.

and

Psyshade is kind of a sickly minded individual in a pervy way

upvote'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trust me, I'm right there with ya. I wrote 1004 with the express thought that it would be deleted rather quick… and it's now my highest rated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious what 890 would think of this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um.
So, in the five days since I posted this, Daniel has become my highest rated SCP. He is also the only thing I've written to get upvotes from both Bright and Yoric, which I am reasonably certain is a sign of the apocalypse. In light of this monumentous occasion, I just wanted to say:
You all confuse the hell out of me;
and Thank you.
Not necessarily in that order.

We now return to your scheduled programing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it works as an inversion, and I would think that it's *obviously* going to seem similar *because* it's an inversion. There has so be some thread of similarity, or it's just an unrelated SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. Carry on, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because the effect isn't particularly complex. Subjects don't perceive anything that isn't actually there- no one sees a face, or hands, or really anything other than a lamp. What the effect does do is instill is the simple, unshakable premise "THIS IS A PERSON", even if that person has to be unplugged and carried along when you want to take him anywhere. Since one does not expect a person to be a lamp under sane circumstances, the discrepany generally doesn't register.

However, a sufficiently observant subject may well notice that something is strange if put in the right situation or posed the right question, and realize that Daniel is, in fact, a lamp; but the impression of personhood is so strong that the realization becomes moot.

Since you're the one to ask, I'm suddenly reminded of your grandmother's "cat" from that one tale.

EDIT: reworded a bit, because I'm incoherent when rambling on a smart phone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Question.

In spite of this effect, it is possible for subjects under the influence of SCP-1040 to be reminded that the object in question is a lamp

If anyone who attempts to discuss him becomes convinced he's a person, how could they do the reminding at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "He must be lonely up there. And space is really cold, right? I should knit him a sweater."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn. I was hoping that the 1040 spot would remain open until I could post something here. What's even more aggravating is this is an incredibly awesome article. Graaagh. +1 to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why I like this entry. I just do. Oh dear, I think I may need to be reassigned..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have a conversation with it until their retinas burn out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's not much the average person can realistically do to injure themselves with the sun, is there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The baby flashlights made me awwwh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least until they start trying to interact with the sun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Several people have mentioned the similarity, and it's an understandable complaint.

As for the inversion, my logic was more to the effect of:
A makes people think they are like the object -> B makes people think the object is like them.

There were several potential ways I could have subverted the concept, but this seemed most workable to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The same way that a radio is the opposite of a vacuum cleaner, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too similar for me to be able to notice it is a inversion. Anthropomorphism and personal identification don't seem to be opposites in my head. Strikes me as a too similar to not a inversion. Not enough to downvote though because it is well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It drives people crazy, but not in an overtly harmful way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get how the effect is opposite, but how exactly is a lamp opposite from a toaster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was thinking, "This is too similar to me."

Then I laughed and upvoted. The idea of people dressing up and acting as if other light-sources are people is what makes this funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As per Bright's challenge, this is my attempt to invert an SCP.

If it wasn't already obvious...
Spoiler =P

SCP-426
